data.body:
## Description

**What**: Create the /dso:onboarding skill with a Socratic dialogue engine that builds holistic project understanding through conversation
**Why**: Replaces three separate rigid wizard skills (project-setup, dev-onboarding, design-onboarding) with a single natural conversation that probes across all understanding areas
**Scope**:
- IN: SKILL.md with Socratic dialogue loop, internal checklist of understanding areas (stack, commands, architecture, infrastructure, CI, design, enforcement preferences), scratchpad file for context protection, SUB-AGENT-GUARD
- OUT: Artifact generation (S3, S4, S5 handle outputs); design-specific questions (S5); detection script changes (S1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, invoking /dso:onboarding starts a Socratic dialogue organized around an internal checklist of understanding areas (stack, commands, architecture, infrastructure, CI, enforcement preferences)
  ← Satisfies: "A single /dso:onboarding skill replaces /dso:project-setup, /dso:dev-onboarding, and /dso:design-onboarding"
- When this story is complete, the skill maintains a scratchpad file (appended to throughout the conversation) so that context window limits do not cause loss of user-provided information
  ← Satisfies: "The skill maintains a scratchpad file"
- When this story is complete, the dialogue uses grounded detection results from S1 to inform questions and pre-fill defaults
  ← Satisfies: "Detection is grounded in filesystem verification"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Conversation-driven skill — test the checklist tracking, scratchpad append behavior, and detection integration rather than conversation content
- [Reliability] Scratchpad must survive context window compression — use append-only writes to a temp file
- [Maintainability] Socratic dialogue pattern will be shared with /dso:architect-foundation (S7) — design the checklist structure for reusability

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
